pages.raw_text:
The threatened split In the building
trades council has come to pass. AT
a meeting of the carpenters union last
night it was unanimously voted to
withdraw their afHliation with the
building trades. This action was no
surprise 10 the balance Of the unions
of the city When the carpenters three
weeks ago refused to endorse the strike
of the plumbers il was then predicted
the action would result in split in
the ranks of the building trades. This
now makes two independent unions in
Spokane, the other being the stone
and brick masons Though not sur
prise to the other unions, the action
is deprecated as it only leads to dis
content among all classes. Next 10
the federal union the carpenters are
the strongest in the city, there being
some 300 members, The building trades
council still comprises the paper-hang-
ers and painters, electricians, plaster
ers, fathers and shinglers, plumbers
and steam and gas filters.
